helpful: Full petition and BIO text under record/documents — the cert-stage arguments drove nearly all case-specific adjustment
Statpack relist/CVSG/per-Term cuts gave clean quantitative anchors
Snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder timeline (amicus count, distribution date) was a strong salience signal
gaps: fedcourts query returned 0 rows on both citation- and topic-based lookups for a modern SCOTUS takings petition; a text/keyword or QP-similarity search over resolved cert petitions would have surfaced comparable priors (e.g. the Lech/Baker/Slaybaugh denials of this same question)
This statpack version has no salience-band table, though the prompt describes one; the band grant-rate would have been the natural anchor for a heavily-amicus'd paid petition
No structured relist tracker for the pending case itself (relists are the classic pre-grant signal but must be inferred from the docket)
notes: Forward cell with a same-day snapshot and full filed-document text; no MCP retrieval was needed. The two corpus queries transferred ~110 MB of ranged reads to return nothing, which suggests citation-overlap ranking is expensive relative to its hit rate on modern SCOTUS dockets.
